[ICategorizedNote](./ts-chocolate.model.icategorizednote.md)


</td><td>

A categorized note associated with an entity.

<tr><td>

[ICategorizedUrl](./ts-chocolate.model.icategorizedurl.md)


</td><td>

A categorized URL for linking to external resources. Used on ingredients, recipes, molds, and confections.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IHasId](./ts-chocolate.model.ihasid.md)


</td><td>

Base interface that option types must extend for use with IOptionsWithPreferred. Enables generic helpers that work with any option type.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IIdsWithPreferred](./ts-chocolate.model.iidswithpreferred.md)


</td><td>

Collection of simple IDs with a preferred selection. Use when options are just IDs without additional metadata.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IMeasurementUnitOption](./ts-chocolate.model.imeasurementunitoption.md)


</td><td>

Option wrapper for measurement units (for use with IOptionsWithPreferred). Wraps a MeasurementUnit to satisfy IHasId requirement.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IOptionsWithPreferred](./ts-chocolate.model.ioptionswithpreferred.md)


</td><td>

Collection of options (objects with IDs) with a preferred selection. Use when options are objects containing IDs plus additional metadata.


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[IRefWithNotes](./ts-chocolate.model.irefwithnotes.md)


</td><td>

Generic reference type with an ID and optional categorized notes. Use as base for mold refs, procedure refs, etc. Satisfies IHasId for use with IOptionsWithPreferred.


</td></tr>
</tbody></table>

</td><td>

Pattern for valid journal base IDs (within a collection) Format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-\[0-9a-f\]<!-- -->{<!-- -->8<!-- -->} Example: "2026-01-15-143025-a1b2c3d4"


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[BASE\_SESSION\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.base_session_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid base session IDs (within a collection) Format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-\[0-9a-f\]<!-- -->{<!-- -->8<!-- -->} Example: "2026-01-15-143025-a1b2c3d4"


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[COMPOSITE\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.composite_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid composite IDs (exactly one dot)


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CONFECTION\_VERSION\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.confection_version_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid confection version IDs Format: confectionId<!-- -->@<!-- -->versionSpec where confectionId is sourceId.baseConfectionId


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[CONFECTION\_VERSION\_SPEC\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.confection_version_spec_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid confection version specs Format: YYYY-MM-DD-NN with optional label (lowercase alphanumeric with dashes) Same pattern as recipe version specs


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FILLING\_VERSION\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.filling_version_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid filling version IDs Format: fillingId<!-- -->@<!-- -->versionSpec where fillingId is sourceId.baseFillingId


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[FILLING\_VERSION\_SPEC\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.filling_version_spec_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid filling version specs Format: YYYY-MM-DD-NN with optional label (lowercase alphanumeric with dashes)


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[ID\_SEPARATOR](./ts-chocolate.model.id_separator.md)


</td><td>

Separator character used in composite IDs


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[JOURNAL\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.journal_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid composite journal IDs Format: collectionId.baseJournalId Example: "user-journals.2026-01-15-143025-a1b2c3d4"


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[SESSION\_ID\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.session_id_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid composite session IDs Format: collectionId.baseSessionId Example: "user-sessions.2026-01-15-143025-a1b2c3d4"


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[SESSION\_SPEC\_PATTERN](./ts-chocolate.model.session_spec_pattern.md)


</td><td>

Pattern for valid session specs Format: YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-\[0-9a-f\]<!-- -->{<!-- -->8<!-- -->} Example: "2026-01-15-143025-a1b2c3d4"


</td></tr>
<tr><td>

[VERSION\_ID\_SEPARATOR](./ts-chocolate.model.version_id_separator.md)


</td><td>

Separator character used in filling version IDs (between FillingId and FillingVersionSpec)


</td></tr>
